fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: New Mexico Dietitian License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a dietitian? Here's whether your license transfers to New Mexico - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: dietitian license reciprocity new mexico, how to transfer dietitian license to new mexico, dietitian compact new mexico, new mexico dietitian endorsement application

board.name: Nutrition and Dietetics Practice Board
board.parent_dept: New Mexico Regulation and Licensing Department
board.mailing_address: RLD - Dietetics and Nutrition Practice Board, P.O. Box 25101, Santa Fe, NM 87504

quick_facts.renewal_cycle: Every 1 year + 15 CE hours or active CDR
quick_facts.ce_requirement: 15 hours per year or active CDR

license_types.title: Licensed Dietitian
license_types.description: Requires current CDR registration (RD/RDN credential), including passage of the CDR examination. As of 2024, CDR requires a minimum master's degree from an ACEND-accredited program.

license_types.title: Licensed Nutritionist
license_types.description: Requires a master's or doctorate in a nutrition-related field from an accredited institution, OR current membership in the ACBN or ASN.

steps.title: Register on the New Mexico Online Licensing System
steps.description: New applicants must first create an account on the RLD's online portal at https://www.rld.nm.gov/online-licensing/ before starting the application process.

steps.title: Complete the Online Application
steps.description: Log in to your newly created account, select the appropriate 'Dietitian' or 'Nutritionist' licensure application, and accurately fill out all required sections within the New Mexico Online Licensing System.

steps.title: Submit Supporting Documentation
steps.description: Upload all necessary documents, which typically include official transcripts from an accredited institution, verification of supervised practice (e.g., ACEND-accredited dietetic internship verification), and official scores from the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR) examination, through the New Mexico Online Licensing System.

steps.title: Pay Application Fee
steps.description: Pay the non-refundable $100.00 application fee online via the New Mexico Online Licensing System using a credit or debit card.

steps.title: Await Board Review and Approval
steps.description: Once the complete application and all required documents and fees are submitted, the Board will review your submission. You can monitor the status of your application directly within the New Mexico Online Licensing System.

documents.item: Copy of current CDR registration card

documents.detail: Must be conducted within the past 90 days per 16.14.3.8 NMAC.
Must be sent directly from the college or university to the Board.

provisional_text: Dietitians and nutritionists who have completed all education and supervised experience requirements but have not yet passed the CDR examination may apply for a provisional permit. This allows practice while awaiting exam results.
reciprocity_text: New Mexico recognizes licensure from other states with standards at least as stringent as its own. Applicants must provide copies of all licenses and arrange for official verification to be sent directly to the Board.

faqs.question: How much does a New Mexico dietitian license cost?
faqs.answer: The total initial cost for a New Mexico dietitian license is $200, broken down as a $50 non-refundable application fee plus a $150 initial license fee. Annual renewal costs $75.

faqs.question: Is a background check required for a New Mexico dietitian license?
faqs.answer: Yes. Per current NMAC regulations, a background check conducted within the past 90 days is required for initial dietitian and nutritionist licensure.

faqs.question: Does New Mexico have reciprocity for dietitians?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers dietitian practice in New Mexico. To work in New Mexico, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to New Mexico?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take Varies (~2 Weeks).

faqs.question: Can I start working in New Mexico while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: New Mexico recognizes licensure from other states with standards at least as stringent as its own. Applicants must provide copies of all licenses and arrange for official verification to be sent directly to the Board.

hero.title: Become a Dietitian in New Mexico
hero.dek: To apply for a Dietitian or Nutritionist license in New Mexico, utilize the New Mexico Online Licensing System provided by the Regulation and Licensing Department.
